verdict.summary: The single-entry feasibility probe observed 2–3 distinct exits while the host route baseline remained unchanged.

hostnameRouting.summary: Hostname-selected routing is not truthful under one bind IP because SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for the current destination-bind-IP selector.
notes: Only one bind IP remains at the shared entry.
SOCKS5 preserves the destination hostname to the remote proxy, not the original shared-entry routing hostname.

protocols.summary: SOCKS5 only works if operators or clients explicitly choose the Mullvad relay, so the product contract would need to change.
protocols.observedCapabilitySummary: SOCKS5 chaining reaches distinct Mullvad exits only when the client explicitly chooses a relay hostname for the second hop.

protocols.summary: HTTP cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
protocols.observedCapabilitySummary: HTTP reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

protocols.summary: HTTPS cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
protocols.observedCapabilitySummary: HTTPS reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

requirementDeltas.title: Hostname-selected location routing
requirementDeltas.summary: Hostname-selected routing no longer truthfully selects the Mullvad exit under the shared-entry topology.
evidence: Hostname-selected routing is not truthful under one bind IP because SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for the current destination-bind-IP selector.

requirementDeltas.title: SOCKS5 support
requirementDeltas.summary: SOCKS5 only works if operators or clients explicitly choose the Mullvad relay, so the product contract would need to change.
evidence: SOCKS5 chaining reaches distinct Mullvad exits only when the client explicitly chooses a relay hostname for the second hop.

requirementDeltas.title: HTTP proxy support
requirementDeltas.summary: HTTP cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
evidence: HTTP reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

requirementDeltas.title: HTTPS proxy support
requirementDeltas.summary: HTTPS cannot truthfully preserve hostname-selected routing under the shared-entry topology.
evidence: HTTPS reaches the shared entry but has no truthful hostname-selected or explicit second-hop selector in the one-entry topology.

requirementDeltas.title: Authenticated proxy access
requirementDeltas.summary: Authenticated proxy access remains visible and enforced at the shared entry.
evidence: Shared-entry probing still requires authenticated local proxy access.

requirementDeltas.title: Low-friction first run for self-hosters
requirementDeltas.summary: The shared-entry topology breaks the low-friction self-hoster story because at least one required protocol no longer works truthfully.
evidence: Operators must configure client-specific relay selection instead of relying on hostname-only routing.

requirementDeltas.title: Easy location naming and discovery
requirementDeltas.summary: Route naming remains diagnosable, but operators must understand an explicit relay-selection mapping instead of hostname-only routing.
evidence: Hostname-selected routing is not truthful under one bind IP because SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for the current destination-bind-IP selector.
Location-selection metadata can still be described to operators in a compatibility bundle.

recommendation.summary: The shared-entry redesign is blocked because one or more active product requirements fail under the compatibility matrix.

notes: Authenticated access remains on the shared entry.
Operators can inspect relay mapping in the compatibility bundle, but clients must opt into explicit relay choice.
Fixture proves that one-entry SOCKS5 chaining can still reach distinct exits while hostname-selected routing fails.
HTTP and HTTPS remain explicit contract failures because no truthful relay selector survives the one-entry topology.
Fixture replay mode skipped all live Mullvad and Docker prerequisites.

summary.headline: SOCKS5 chaining can still work with explicit relay selection, but hostname-selected routing fails and HTTP/HTTPS cannot keep Mullgate’s current truthful contract: Hostname-selected routing fails under the one-entry topology because the shared entry collapses to one bind IP and SOCKS5 does not preserve the requested proxy hostname for destination-bind-IP selection.
